title: Public Testimony on Child Lead Poisoning Rates, North Side Water Pressure Incident, and CIP Funding
summary: Oceanceana Fair (Families for Lead Freedom) testifies that approximately 600 children per year are poisoned by lead in Onondaga County -- equivalent to filling McKinley Brighton School. Primary source is lead paint, but water exposure adds risk at lower levels: NY State action level is 5.5 micrograms/deciliter vs CDC's 3.5, and newer studies show lower-level exposure is more damaging. Mandatory testing at ages 1 and 2 per NY State well-child check; children 5 and under at highest risk. Chair Majok commits to offline coordination on education strategy. North side water pressure incident raised by Councilor Nave -- very low pressure two weeks prior, possible Aqua connection; concern that low-flow water cannot be flushed adequately. Commissioner disagrees, says flowing water is fresh regardless of pressure. Peter Kings (community member) presents water transient theory: 20% non-revenue water (from 2000 report), shifting soils cause pipe movement, low/no-pressure events could pull soil contaminants into water via water hammer effect -- a potentially underexplored mechanism. Commissioner presents funding: $25M Capital Improvement Plan over 6 years for water infrastructure and lead service line replacements; $22,756,047 one-time Bipartisan Infrastructure Law lead service line grant (deemed safe from federal clawback).
